title: Argus

jokes.text: I just got my bridesmaid's dress For cerie's wedding. It is a vietnamese size two.
jokes.setup: Liz discussing her bridesmaid dress
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific 'Vietnamese size two' designation
jokes.explanation: Specific, absurd cultural reference that feels authentic to character's neuroses about body image

jokes.text: Do you know what it's like when a younger man shows up Wearing the same cufflinks? You might as well be invisible.
jokes.setup: Jack claiming dressing up isn't easier for men
jokes.punchline: The hilariously specific rich-person problem of matching cufflinks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy absurd elitism - only he would have this specific wealthy person anxiety

jokes.text: - A suicide cult! - A gym.
jokes.setup: Jack guessing what Liz joined
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic reveal that it's just a gym after the dramatic 'suicide cult'
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with perfect economy - maximum contrast in minimal words

jokes.text: If the will says that you have to spend the night In a haunted house, you better hope That everybody else there is black guys and sluts.
jokes.setup: Liz giving Jack advice about the will
jokes.punchline: Reference to horror movie tropes about who survives
jokes.explanation: Relies on familiar horror trope knowledge, somewhat cheap but delivered well

jokes.text: Maybe that boyhood sled he held so dear. I believed he called it... Sleddy.
jokes.setup: Jack wanting something meaningful from Don's estate
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic name 'Sleddy' for what's clearly a Citizen Kane reference
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of iconic Citizen Kane reference with deliberately mundane name

jokes.text: any chance it's one of those Bendy hospital beds? Maybe. He did have three.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about what Don left behind
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation that Don Geiss had three bendy hospital beds
jokes.explanation: Absurd wealthy person excess delivered casually, good escalation from one to three

jokes.text: Life is like tv. Testing tells us that people like weddings, births, And episodes where a character dies.
jokes.setup: Tracy responding to wedding announcement
jokes.punchline: Meta commentary on TV tropes while being in a TV show
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta humor that works on multiple levels - Tracy's showbiz knowledge plus 30 Rock's self-awareness

jokes.text: My heart! [reacting to Tracy's comment about character deaths]
jokes.setup: Tracy just mentioned episodes where characters die
jokes.punchline: Someone immediately fakes a heart attack
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with great timing and economy

jokes.text: You sound like my mother Talking to the planned parenthood lady.
jokes.setup: Grizz being indecisive about his best man
jokes.punchline: Tracy's specific and inappropriate comparison to his mother
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's very Tracy-specific, though walks line of being purely shock value

jokes.text: You sound like my mother being pulled onstage At a 2 live crew concert.
jokes.setup: Following up his previous mother comparison
jokes.punchline: Even more specific and absurd mother scenario
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that builds on the previous joke with increasingly specific scenarios

jokes.text: My mother had problems.
jokes.setup: Tracy's elaborate descriptions of his mother's situations
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement after the extreme scenarios
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan understatement after escalating absurdity - great economy and timing

jokes.text: jack is 'bros' with lamar odom
jokes.setup: Explaining why they can't do the Kardashian sketch
jokes.punchline: Jack Donaghy being 'bros' with anyone, especially a reality TV figure
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - formal Jack being 'bros' with reality TV star

jokes.text: Are you soaking your tampo in vodka again?
jokes.setup: Pete questioning Jenna's unusually happy behavior
jokes.punchline: The specific and disturbing explanation he offers
jokes.explanation: Shocking specificity but relies somewhat on grossout factor rather than pure wit

jokes.text: Remember when she dated that sniper?
jokes.setup: Discussing Jenna's terrible dating history
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of dating a sniper as just another bad relationship
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of 'bad boyfriend' that feels authentic to Jenna's chaos

jokes.text: Jenna, there's a laser sight on your forehead. Oh, please. He's not gonna fire. For god's sakes, he's scared of his own mother! Aren't cha, alan?
jokes.setup: The sniper boyfriend situation
jokes.punchline: Jenna's casual dismissal of mortal danger and emasculating the sniper
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - only Jenna would casually dismiss a sniper while emasculating him

jokes.text: Pete, that was, like, two years ago. It just won't heal.
jokes.setup: Discussing Jenna's bite from a previous breakup
jokes.punchline: Pete's wound from Jenna's bite still hasn't healed after two years
jokes.explanation: Good escalation suggesting Jenna bit him so hard it's a chronic condition

jokes.text: His even lovelier mistress. The secret canadian family. And his even more secret attic family.
jokes.setup: Lawyer listing Don Geiss's beneficiaries
jokes.punchline: Escalating levels of secret families, including one in the attic
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with increasingly absurd secret families, great economy

jokes.text: the most important of which Is to not let the piece get wet.
jokes.setup: Instructions for caring for the inherited pocket watch
jokes.punchline: Clear Gremlins reference applied to a pocket watch
jokes.explanation: Straightforward pop culture reference without much additional twist or character integration

jokes.text: Because of our sexual past. Leave it open. I'd feel safer.
jokes.setup: Grizz asking if wedding talk was awkward for Liz
jokes.punchline: Liz's panicked response to Grizz's oversharing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Liz's practical safety response to awkward revelation

jokes.text: We really were the sam and diane of this place.
jokes.setup: Grizz saying Liz knows his every thought
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself and Liz to classic TV romantic couple
jokes.explanation: Solid character-specific reference that plays into the show's comedy writer inside jokes

jokes.text: But you can call me dallas. [Pete introducing himself]
jokes.setup: Pete giving his normal introduction
jokes.punchline: Randomly adding an unnecessary nickname
jokes.explanation: Odd character moment that suggests Pete's desperate attempt to seem cool

jokes.text: Through a mutual fund. Friend, jenna. Oh, of course. Through a friend fund.
jokes.setup: Paul explaining how he met Jenna
jokes.punchline: The misheard 'friend fund' and Liz's confused agreement
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay with good setup but not particularly memorable or character-specific

jokes.text: Oh, I work for a bank. -...Rupt circus. He works for a bankrupt circus.
jokes.setup: Paul starting to explain his job normally
jokes.punchline: Jenna's interruption creating 'bankrupt circus' from 'bank'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect timing and misdirection, very economical

jokes.text: Already-married cat strangler.
jokes.setup: Liz guessing what Paul is hiding
jokes.punchline: Specific and escalating criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character paranoia from Liz but relies on shock value combination

jokes.text: Agh! Living dinosaur!
jokes.setup: Someone seeing the peacock for the first time
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overreaction to a peacock
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with good economy but not particularly sophisticated

jokes.text: Peacocks can live up to 40 years, Longer if they're not part of mike tyson's home zoo.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining peacock lifespans
jokes.punchline: Reference to Mike Tyson's notorious pet ownership
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference with dark implication but somewhat easy target

jokes.text: How to avoid getting paper cuts while making love On a pile of money.
jokes.setup: Jack describing conversations with Don Geiss
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific rich person problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack/Don absurd wealth humor - incredibly specific and character-appropriate

jokes.text: We three peacocks felt like we owned the world.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his relationship with Don and the peacock
jokes.punchline: Jack including himself as one of the 'three peacocks'
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Jack's ego makes him equal to Don while including a literal peacock

jokes.text: Like we might someday, j-town?
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning he and Don had nicknames
jokes.punchline: Liz's awkward attempt to establish a nickname relationship
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's social awkwardness and desire for connection

jokes.text: Terms we learned on a trip to japan Because they played the movie rising sun on the airplane.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the Japanese terms he and Don used
jokes.punchline: Their 'cultural knowledge' came from an airplane movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Jack's pretentious cultural reference - very character appropriate

jokes.text: Oh, his feather went in my mouth. Ew, it's so oily. Lemon, he's marked you. He thinks you're his wife.
jokes.setup: Liz getting too close to the peacock
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact explanation of peacock mating behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from disgusting to absurd with Jack's authoritative delivery

jokes.text: The wedding band is u2? No! You two idiots!
jokes.setup: Tracy excited about the wedding band being U2
jokes.punchline: Liz's clarification that she meant 'you two' not the band U2
jokes.explanation: Classic wordplay with perfect setup and great economy, though somewhat obvious

jokes.text: I've known you since you was six feet tall!
jokes.setup: Tracy emphasizing his long friendship with Grizz
jokes.punchline: Using height instead of age as a time reference, implying Grizz is much taller now
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that works perfectly for Tracy's character and Grizz's size

jokes.text: and because of you, I'm having a tantrum! Now pick up that table and smash it for me!
jokes.setup: Tracy getting angry
jokes.punchline: Tracy outsourcing his own tantrum destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment - self-aware of his tantrum but still entitled enough to delegate it

jokes.text: I'm just feeling the weight Of jenna's jewelry on my finger pads.
jokes.setup: Paul explaining what he's doing
jokes.punchline: Creepily intimate familiarity with Jenna's jewelry
jokes.explanation: Unsettling character detail that hints at his obsession, though somewhat obvious

jokes.text: They couldn't find cowboy hats big enough For my kid's head, So they're just wearing turbans.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his kids' school play
jokes.punchline: The absurd costume substitution due to head size
jokes.explanation: Great Pete self-deprecation with absurd practical solution, good visual

jokes.text: Words are the first step on the road to deeds!
jokes.setup: Liz psyching herself up for the gym
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top motivational speak for basic gym attendance
jokes.explanation: Good Liz character moment showing her tendency to over-intellectualize simple things

jokes.text: Oh, my, what a gorgeous swamp eagle.
jokes.setup: Kenneth seeing Argus the peacock
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's completely wrong identification of the bird
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - confidently wrong but with poetic phrasing

jokes.text: He lives...Or you die.
jokes.setup: Jack giving Kenneth responsibility for the peacock
jokes.punchline: Dramatically high stakes for pet care
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack dramatic overstatement with great economy and threat escalation

jokes.text: I was about to suggest the same thing.
jokes.setup: Jack's death threat if the peacock dies
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's cheerful agreement with his own potential execution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - bizarre cheerfulness about his own death

jokes.text: Before I show you the back of my hand.
jokes.setup: Tracy threatening someone
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned threat that doesn't match his modern persona
jokes.explanation: Decent character inconsistency humor but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: He's in love with grizz's fiancee... Feyonce! Oh, your stutter is back. No, grizz's fiancee's name is feyonce. Like beyonce with an 'f.'
jokes.setup: Tracy seeming to stutter on 'fiancée'
jokes.punchline: Reveal that the woman's name is actually 'Feyoncé'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that creates perfect character name pun, very quotable

jokes.text: You think that's impressive, Watch me stand on one foot. Hang on. I did it earlier.
jokes.setup: Tracy wanting to show off
jokes.punchline: Failing at the most basic physical task
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy that fits Tracy's childlike incompetence

jokes.text: * my muffin top is all that * * whole grain, low fat *
jokes.setup: Paul performing as Jenna
jokes.punchline: Singing Jenna's actual song from earlier in the series
jokes.explanation: Great callback that works on multiple levels - Paul's obsession and series continuity

jokes.text: Jenna's boyfriend is a jenna maroney impersonator.
jokes.setup: Building mystery about Paul's secret
jokes.punchline: The absurdly narcissistic revelation
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect Jenna storyline - peak narcissism that's somehow believable for her character

jokes.text: Let me retell it in slow motion.
jokes.setup: Jack defending his grief process
jokes.punchline: Dramatically slowing down his non-reaction
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the previous joke with perfect character consistency

jokes.text: What if he rolls over on his back And his cloaca fills with mucous?
jokes.setup: Jack worried about leaving Argus alone
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific bird anatomy concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack over-research anxiety with disgustingly specific bird knowledge

jokes.text: Well, not new york thin, but--
jokes.setup: Liz saying she's stretched thin
jokes.punchline: Qualifying that she's not actually thin
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating Liz moment with nice economy and character consistency

jokes.text: Sorry--mrs. Argus.
jokes.setup: Kenneth addressing Liz
jokes.punchline: Referring to her as the peacock's wife
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Kenneth's literal interpretation of animal behavior

jokes.text: Oh, no, I'm white. I can't read that word.
jokes.setup: Liz reading Grizz's letter
jokes.punchline: Awkward racial self-awareness about saying certain words
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's liberal anxiety in uncomfortable territory

jokes.text: Your judgmental badger face.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why she didn't tell Liz about Paul
jokes.punchline: Specific animal comparison for Liz's expression
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult that's both accurate and oddly specific

jokes.text: ooh, that's a car alarm.
jokes.setup: Kenneth interpreting peacock sounds
jokes.punchline: Identifying a completely wrong sound
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment with his confident wrongness

jokes.text: Or how bread turns into toast.
jokes.setup: Kenneth discussing life's mysteries
jokes.punchline: Including basic toasting as an incomprehensible mystery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - treating mundane process as divine mystery

jokes.text: badger. It's another badger. The third badger has taken the bait. Why is everyone code named badger?
jokes.setup: Spy operation with code names
jokes.punchline: Everyone having the same code name defeats the purpose
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist spy parody that highlights their incompetence

jokes.text: I'd never tuck my penis again if she asked me.
jokes.setup: Paul explaining his devotion to Jenna
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific sacrifice he'd make for love
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but character-appropriate declaration of devotion

jokes.text: She finally gets to love herself.
jokes.setup: Liz realizing what Jenna gets from the relationship
jokes.punchline: The psychological breakthrough about narcissistic fulfillment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant psychological insight that makes the absurd relationship make perfect sense

jokes.text: Damn, beth, let it go.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning their sexual past again
jokes.punchline: Grizz's exasperated response to her fixation
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Grizz is tired of Liz bringing up their past

tmdb_overview: Wedding fever hits TGS, leaving Liz to sort out Grizz's dilemma when both Tracy and Dot Com want to be his best man. Jenna's new boyfriend, Paul, raises Liz and Pete's suspicions, while Jack is finally forced to deal with the death of his mentor, Don Geiss, when Geiss leaves him a strange but meaningful inheritance.
insight: Absurdist escalation drives 57 jokes across 42 minutes—peak Season 4 density.
